title: Payments Team Standup - April 7
discussion_points: PAY-210 (P0): James reported the international payments null currency bug. ~150 failed transactions/day. He has the root cause identified and will have a PR up today.
PAY-189: Sarah's idempotent retry PR #247 has one approval from Marcus. Elena requested changes on the idempotency key expiration edge case. Design review scheduled for 11am today.
PAY-211: Alex is working through the Stripe v12 upgrade. CI is failing on webhook handler tests - needs to update test fixtures for the new event format.
PAY-214: Marcus picked up the webhook delivery reliability issue. Will start after the design review.
PAY-215: Priya has the volume endpoint done for the analytics API. Working on success rate and latency endpoints.
decisions: PAY-210 is top priority - James to have fix ready for review by EOD.
Design review for PAY-189 idempotency key approach at 11am in Conference Room B.

action_items.description: Submit PR for PAY-210 null currency fix
Address Elena's feedback on PR #247 key expiration handling
Update Stripe webhook test fixtures for v12 format
Start on webhook retry queue implementation (PAY-214)

title: Checkout Team Standup - April 7
discussion_points: CHK-45: Elena's checkout wizard PR #112 has approval from Priya but James requested accessibility changes. Focus management between steps and aria-labels on the progress indicator.
CHK-48: James's Apple Pay / Google Pay PR #114 is approved by Elena. Ready to merge.
CHK-49: Priya is working on guest checkout session management. Expects a PR by end of week.
CHK-52: Elena found that the shipping form loses state on browser back. Opened a draft PR #116 with a session storage fix.
decisions: CHK-48 (Apple Pay/Google Pay) to be merged once CI passes.
Elena to address a11y feedback on checkout wizard before end of sprint.

action_items.description: Add aria-labels and focus management to checkout wizard (PR #112)
Merge Apple Pay / Google Pay PR #114 after CI

title: Sprint 24/12 Planning - April 6
discussion_points: Reviewed Payments Sprint 23 results: 32/38 points completed (84%). Carry-over: PAY-189 (idempotent retries, 8pts) still in review.
Reviewed Checkout Sprint 11 results: 26/26 points completed (100%). Great sprint!
Payments Sprint 24 goal: Ship idempotent retries (PAY-189), fix P0 international payments bug (PAY-210), begin analytics API (PAY-215).
Checkout Sprint 12 goal: Launch checkout wizard redesign (CHK-45), ship wallet payments (CHK-48), implement guest checkout (CHK-49).
New P0 bug PAY-210 discovered over the weekend - international payments failing with null currency. James volunteered to take it.
PAY-216 (rate limiting) added to Sprint 24 but currently unassigned. Alex may pick it up after Stripe upgrade.
Team capacity discussion: Elena is over-committed (16pts vs 13 capacity). James and Priya are also slightly over. Need to watch burnout.
decisions: Payments Sprint 24 committed to 42 points (ambitious given velocity average of ~30).
PAY-210 is the top priority for the Payments team.
PAY-216 will be assigned once someone frees up capacity.

action_items.description: Take on PAY-210 P0 bug as top priority
Finalize PAY-189 idempotent retry PR based on review feedback
Assess if CHK-52 can be deferred to reduce overcommitment
Draft analytics API endpoint spec for design review

title: Payment Retry Design Review - April 7
discussion_points: Reviewed Sarah's idempotency key design in PR #247. Uses Redis-backed store with 24h TTL.
Elena raised concern: if a transaction takes >24h to fully settle and the key expires, a retry could create a duplicate. Edge case but possible for international wire transfers.
Discussed options: (a) extend TTL to 72h, (b) tie key expiration to transaction settlement status, (c) use database-backed keys with no TTL.
Marcus suggested a hybrid: Redis for fast lookups + database for persistence. Keys remain in Redis for 24h but are always backed by the DB.
Alex pointed out that the retry backoff strategy should use jitter to avoid thundering herd on recovery from outages.
decisions: Adopt hybrid approach: Redis cache + database persistence for idempotency keys.
Key TTL in Redis: 24h. Database records: permanent (with cleanup job for records >90 days old).
Add jitter to the exponential backoff in retry logic.

action_items.description: Update PR #247 with hybrid Redis+DB idempotency key storage
Add jitter to retry backoff implementation

title: Sarah / James 1:1 - April 7
discussion_points: James is progressing well on the PAY-210 P0 fix. Root cause is simple - null check missing in the validator.
Discussed James's cross-team role. He's splitting time between Payments (PAY-210) and Checkout (CHK-48 Apple Pay). Currently at 11 points vs 10 capacity.
James interested in taking on more backend architecture work. Sarah will look for opportunities in upcoming sprints.
Talked about the checkout wizard a11y review - James wants to champion accessibility practices across both teams.
decisions: After PAY-210 is merged, James will focus on CHK-48 merge and then the checkout wizard a11y review.
Sarah will include James in architecture discussions for the multi-currency settlement feature (PAY-213) next sprint.

action_items.description: Complete PAY-210 fix and get it merged
Loop James into PAY-213 architecture planning
